verses.text: Solomon saw the tears of the oppressed with 'no comforter,' and the power on the oppressors' side. Such injustice grieved him deeply. A world of unrelieved oppression made him count the dead happier than the living.

Solomon saw the tears of the oppressed with 'no comforter,' and the power on the oppressors' side. Such injustice grieved him deeply. A world of unrelieved oppression made him count the dead happier than the living.

Solomon saw the tears of the oppressed with 'no comforter,' and the power on the oppressors' side. Such injustice grieved him deeply. A world of unrelieved oppression made him count the dead happier than the living.

He saw that skilful work springs often from envy of one's neighbour, mere vanity; 'better is an handful with quietness, than both the hands full with travail.' Restless striving is vanity. A little held in peace is better than much won by anxious rivalry.

He noted the lonely man, without family, who toils endlessly yet asks, 'For whom do I labour?' Wealth without love is empty. To gather and have no one to share with is a sore vanity.
He noted the lonely man, without family, who toils endlessly yet asks, 'For whom do I labour?' Wealth without love is empty. To gather and have no one to share with is a sore vanity.
'Two are better than one,' for they help, warm, and defend each other, and 'a threefold cord is not quickly broken.' Fellowship is a gift. We are made for companionship, stronger together than alone.

Better a poor wise youth than an old foolish king who will heed no counsel; yet even the new favourite is soon forgotten. Popularity is fleeting. The crowd's praise passes from one to the next, and is itself vanity.
